text: One year of recreational endurance training at 60% heart rate reserve, 3 days/week for 45 min/session, significantly reduces body fat by approximately 3.4% without changing body weight in previously sedentary adults.
Resting metabolic rate does not change significantly after 12 months of moderate-intensity aerobic endurance training in previously untrained non-obese individuals.
Substrate oxidation at rest does not change significantly after one year of moderate-intensity endurance training in previously untrained individuals.
Maximal fat oxidation rate during exercise increases significantly and progressively over at least 12 months of consistent moderate-intensity endurance training at 60% heart rate reserve.
The exercise intensity at which maximal fat oxidation occurs shifts progressively higher with training, increasing from 35% to 50% VO2max over 12 months of moderate endurance training.

A constant moderate training stimulus within ACSM guidelines (3×45 min/week at 60% HRR) continues to elicit sustained metabolic improvements in fat oxidation for at least one year without requiring progressive overload.
